695: static void xmt_remove_link_for_descriptor(GF_XMTParser* parser, GF_Descriptor* desc) {
696: 
697: 	u32 i=0;
698: 	XMT_ODLink *l, *to_del=NULL;
699: 	while ((l = (XMT_ODLink*)gf_list_enum(parser->od_links, &i)) ) {
700: 		if (l->od && l->od == (GF_ObjectDescriptor*)desc) {
701: 			l->od = NULL;
702: 			to_del = l;
703: 			break;
704: 		}
705: 	}
706: 	if (to_del) {
707: 
708: 		i=0;
709: 		GF_Descriptor* subdesc;
710: 		while ((subdesc = gf_list_enum(((GF_ObjectDescriptor*)desc)->ESDescriptors, &i))) {
711: 			if (subdesc) xmt_remove_link_for_descriptor(parser, subdesc);
712: 		}
713: 
714: 		gf_list_del_item(parser->od_links, to_del);
715: 		if (to_del->desc_name) gf_free(to_del->desc_name);
716: 		gf_list_del(to_del->mf_urls);
717: 		gf_free(to_del);
718: 	}
719: 
720: 	XMT_ESDLink *esdl, *esdl_del=NULL;
721: 	i=0;
722: 	while ((esdl = (XMT_ESDLink *)gf_list_enum(parser->esd_links, &i))) {
723: 		if (esdl->esd && esdl->esd == (GF_ESD*)desc) {
724: 			esdl->esd = NULL;
725: 			esdl_del = esdl;
726: 			break;
727: 		}
728: 	}
729: 
730: 	if (esdl_del) {
731: 		gf_list_del_item(parser->esd_links, esdl_del);
732: 		if (esdl_del->desc_name) gf_free(esdl_del->desc_name);
733: 		gf_free(esdl_del);
734: 	}
735: 
736: }
737: 
738: static void xmt_node_end(void *sax_cbck, const char *name, const char *name_space)
739: {
740: 	u32 tag;
741: 	GF_XMTParser *parser = (GF_XMTParser *)sax_cbck;
742: 	XMTNodeStack *top;
743: 	GF_Descriptor *desc;
744: 	GF_Node *node = NULL;
745: 	if (!parser->doc_type || !parser->state) return;
746: 
747: 	top = (XMTNodeStack *)gf_list_last(parser->nodes);
748: 
749: 	if (!top) {
750: 		/*check descr*/
751: 		desc = (GF_Descriptor*)gf_list_last(parser->descriptors);
752: 		if (desc && (desc->tag == gf_odf_get_tag_by_name((char *)name)) ) {
753: 
754: 			/*assign timescales once the ESD has been parsed*/
755: 			if (desc->tag == GF_ODF_ESD_TAG) {
756: 				GF_ESD *esd = (GF_ESD*)desc;
757: 				GF_StreamContext *sc = gf_sm_stream_new(parser->load->ctx, esd->ESID, esd->decoderConfig ? esd->decoderConfig->streamType : 0, esd->decoderConfig ? esd->decoderConfig->objectTypeIndication : 0);
758: 				if (sc && esd->slConfig && esd->slConfig->timestampResolution)
759: 					sc->timeScale = esd->slConfig->timestampResolution;
760: 			}
761: 
762: 			gf_list_rem_last(parser->descriptors);
763: 			if (gf_list_count(parser->descriptors)) return;
764: 
765: 			if ((parser->doc_type==1) && (parser->state==XMT_STATE_HEAD) && parser->load->ctx && !parser->load->ctx->root_od) {
766: 				parser->load->ctx->root_od = (GF_ObjectDescriptor *)desc;
767: 			}
768: 			else if (!parser->od_command) {
769: 				xmt_report(parser, GF_OK, "Warning: descriptor %s defined outside scene scope - skipping", name);
770: 				xmt_remove_link_for_descriptor(parser, desc);
771: 				gf_odf_desc_del(desc);
772: 			} else {
773: 				switch (parser->od_command->tag) {
774: 				case GF_ODF_ESD_UPDATE_TAG:
775: 					gf_list_add( ((GF_ESDUpdate *)parser->od_command)->ESDescriptors, desc);
776: 					break;
777: 				/*same struct for OD update and IPMP update*/
778: 				case GF_ODF_OD_UPDATE_TAG:
779: 				case GF_ODF_IPMP_UPDATE_TAG:
780: 					gf_list_add( ((GF_ODUpdate *)parser->od_command)->objectDescriptors, desc);
781: 					break;
782: 				}
783: 			}
784: 
785: 			return;
786: 		}
787: 		if (parser->state == XMT_STATE_HEAD) {
788: 			if ((parser->doc_type == 1) && !strcmp(name, "Header")) parser->state = XMT_STATE_BODY;
789: 			/*X3D header*/
790: 			else if ((parser->doc_type == 2) && !strcmp(name, "head")) {
791: 				parser->state = XMT_STATE_BODY;
792: 				/*create a group at root level*/
793: 				tag = xmt_get_node_tag(parser, "Group");
794: 				node = gf_node_new(parser->load->scene_graph, tag);
795: 				gf_node_register(node, NULL);
796: 				gf_sg_set_root_node(parser->load->scene_graph, node);
797: 				gf_node_init(node);
798: 
799: 				/*create a default top for X3D*/
800: 				GF_SAFEALLOC(parser->x3d_root, XMTNodeStack);
801: 				if (!parser->x3d_root) {
802: 					GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_PARSER, ("Failed to allocate X3D root\n"));
803: 					return;
804: 				}
805: 				parser->x3d_root->node = node;
806: 			}
807: 			/*XMT-O header*/
808: 			else if ((parser->doc_type == 3) && !strcmp(name, "head")) parser->state = XMT_STATE_BODY;
809: 		}
810: 		else if (parser->state == XMT_STATE_ELEMENTS) {
811: 			gf_assert((parser->doc_type != 1) || parser->command);
812: 			if (!strcmp(name, "Replace") || !strcmp(name, "Insert") || !strcmp(name, "Delete")) {
813: 				parser->command = NULL;
814: 				parser->state = XMT_STATE_COMMANDS;
815: 			}
816: 			else if (!strcmp(name, "repField")) {
817: 				parser->state = XMT_STATE_COMMANDS;
818: 			}
819: 			/*end proto*/
820: 			else if (!strcmp(name, "ProtoDeclare") || !strcmp(name, "ExternProtoDeclare"))  {
821: 				GF_Proto *cur = parser->parsing_proto;
822: 				xmt_resolve_routes(parser);
823: 				parser->parsing_proto = (GF_Proto*)cur->userpriv;
824: 				parser->load->scene_graph = cur->parent_graph;
825: 				cur->userpriv = NULL;
826: 			}
827: 			else if (parser->proto_field && !strcmp(name, "field")) parser->proto_field = NULL;
828: 			/*end X3D body*/
829: 			else if ((parser->doc_type == 2) && !strcmp(name, "Scene")) parser->state = XMT_STATE_BODY_END;
830: 		}
831: 		else if (parser->state == XMT_STATE_COMMANDS) {
832: 			/*end XMT-A body*/
833: 			if ((parser->doc_type == 1) && !strcmp(name, "Body")) parser->state = XMT_STATE_BODY_END;
834: 			/*end X3D body*/
835: 			else if ((parser->doc_type == 2) && !strcmp(name, "Scene")) parser->state = XMT_STATE_BODY_END;
836: 			/*end XMT-O body*/
837: 			else if ((parser->doc_type == 3) && !strcmp(name, "body")) parser->state = XMT_STATE_BODY_END;
838: 
839: 			/*end scene command*/
840: 			else if (!strcmp(name, "Replace") || !strcmp(name, "Insert") || !strcmp(name, "Delete") )  {
841: 				/*restore parent command if in CommandBuffer*/
842: 				if (parser->command && parser->command_buffer && parser->command_buffer->buffer) {
843: 					//empty <Insert>
844: 					if ((parser->command->tag==GF_SG_ROUTE_INSERT) && !parser->command->fromNodeID) {
845: 						gf_list_del_item(parser->command_buffer->commandList, parser->command);
846: 					}
847: 
848: 					parser->command = (GF_Command*) parser->command_buffer->buffer;
849: 					parser->command_buffer->buffer = NULL;
850: 					parser->command_buffer = NULL;
851: 				} else {
852: 					//empty <Insert>
853: 					if (parser->command && (parser->command->tag==GF_SG_ROUTE_INSERT) && !parser->command->fromNodeID) {
854: 						gf_list_del_item(parser->scene_au->commands, parser->command);
855: 					}
856: 					parser->command = NULL;
857: 				}
858: 			}
859: 			/*end OD command*/
860: 			else if (!strcmp(name, "ObjectDescriptorUpdate") || !strcmp(name, "ObjectDescriptorRemove")
861: 			         || !strcmp(name, "ES_DescriptorUpdate") || !strcmp(name, "ES_DescriptorRemove")
862: 			         || !strcmp(name, "IPMP_DescriptorUpdate") || !strcmp(name, "IPMP_DescriptorRemove") ) {
863: 				parser->od_command = NULL;
864: 			}
865: 
866: 			else if (!strcmp(name, "par"))
867: 				parser->in_com = 1;
868: 
869: 
870: 		}
871: 		else if (parser->state == XMT_STATE_BODY_END) {
872: 			/*end XMT-A*/
873: 			if ((parser->doc_type == 1) && !strcmp(name, "XMT-A")) parser->state = XMT_STATE_END;
874: 			/*end X3D*/
875: 			else if ((parser->doc_type == 2) && !strcmp(name, "X3D")) {
876: 				while (1) {
877: 					GF_Node *n = (GF_Node *)gf_list_last(parser->script_to_load);
878: 					if (!n) break;
879: 					gf_list_rem_last(parser->script_to_load);
880: 					gf_sg_script_load(n);
881: 				}
882: 				gf_list_del(parser->script_to_load);
883: 				parser->script_to_load = NULL;
884: 				parser->state = XMT_STATE_END;
885: 			}
886: 			/*end XMT-O*/
887: 			else if ((parser->doc_type == 3) && !strcmp(name, "XMT-O")) parser->state = XMT_STATE_END;
888: 		}
889: 		return;
890: 	}
891: 	/*only remove created nodes ... */
892: 	tag = xmt_get_node_tag(parser, name);
893: 	if (!tag) {
894: 		if (top->container_field.name) {
895: 			if (!strcmp(name, top->container_field.name)) {
896: 				if (top->container_field.fieldType==GF_SG_VRML_SFCOMMANDBUFFER) {
897: 					parser->state = XMT_STATE_ELEMENTS;
898: 					parser->command = (GF_Command *) (void *) parser->command_buffer->buffer;
899: 					parser->command_buffer->buffer = NULL;
900: 					parser->command_buffer = NULL;
901: 				}
902: 				top->container_field.far_ptr = NULL;
903: 				top->container_field.name = NULL;
904: 				top->last = NULL;
905: 			}
906: 			/*end of command inside an command (conditional.buffer replace)*/
907: 			else if (!strcmp(name, "Replace") || !strcmp(name, "Insert") || !strcmp(name, "Delete") )  {
908: 				if (parser->command_buffer) {
909: 					if (parser->command_buffer->bufferSize) {
910: 						parser->command_buffer->bufferSize--;
911: 					} else {
912: 						SFCommandBuffer *prev = (SFCommandBuffer *) parser->command_buffer->buffer;
913: 						parser->command_buffer->buffer = NULL;
914: 						parser->command_buffer = prev;
915: 					}
916: 					/*stay in command parsing mode (state 3) until we find </buffer>*/
917: 					parser->state = XMT_STATE_COMMANDS;
918: 				}
919: 			}
920: 			/*end of protofield node(s) content*/
921: 			else if (!strcmp(name, "node") || !strcmp(name, "nodes")) {
922: 				top->container_field.far_ptr = NULL;
923: 				top->container_field.name = NULL;
924: 				top->last = NULL;
925: 			}
926: 		}
927: 		/*SF/MFNode proto field, just pop node stack*/
928: 		else if (!top->node && !strcmp(name, "field")) {
929: 			gf_list_rem_last(parser->nodes);
930: 			gf_free(top);
931: 		} else if (top->node && top->node->sgprivate->tag == TAG_ProtoNode) {
932: 			if (!strcmp(name, "node") || !strcmp(name, "nodes")) {
933: 				top->container_field.far_ptr = NULL;
934: 				top->container_field.name = NULL;
935: 				top->last = NULL;
936: 			} else if (!strcmp(name, "ProtoInstance")) {
937: 				gf_list_rem_last(parser->nodes);
938: 				node = top->node;
939: 				gf_free(top);
940: 				goto attach_node;
941: 			}
942: 		}
943: 	} else if (top->node->sgprivate->tag==tag) {
944: 		node = top->node;
945: 		gf_list_rem_last(parser->nodes);
946: 		gf_free(top);
947: 
948: attach_node:
949: 		top = (XMTNodeStack*)gf_list_last(parser->nodes);
950: 		/*add node to command*/
951: 		if (!top || (top->container_field.fieldType==GF_SG_VRML_SFCOMMANDBUFFER)) {
952: 			if (parser->doc_type == 1) {
953: 				GF_CommandField *inf;
954: 				Bool single_node = 0;
955: 				if (!parser->command) {
956: 					gf_assert(0);
957: 					return;
958: 				}
959: 				switch (parser->command->tag) {
960: 				case GF_SG_SCENE_REPLACE:
961: 					if (parser->parsing_proto) {
962: 						gf_sg_proto_add_node_code(parser->parsing_proto, node);
963: 						gf_node_register(node, NULL);
964: 					} else if (!parser->command->node) {
965: 						parser->command->node = node;
966: 						gf_node_register(node, NULL);
967: 					} else if (parser->command->node != node) {
968: 						xmt_report(parser, GF_OK, "Warning: top-node already assigned - discarding node %s", name);
969: 						gf_node_register(node, NULL);
970: 						gf_node_unregister(node, NULL);
971: 					}
972: 					break;
973: 				case GF_SG_GLOBAL_QUANTIZER:
974: 				case GF_SG_NODE_INSERT:
975: 				case GF_SG_INDEXED_INSERT:
976: 				case GF_SG_INDEXED_REPLACE:
977: 					single_node = 1;
978: 				case GF_SG_NODE_REPLACE:
979: 				case GF_SG_FIELD_REPLACE:
980: 				case GF_SG_MULTIPLE_REPLACE:
981: 					inf = (GF_CommandField*)gf_list_last(parser->command->command_fields);
982: 					if (!inf) {
983: 						inf = gf_sg_command_field_new(parser->command);
984: 						inf->fieldType = GF_SG_VRML_SFNODE;
985: 					}
986: 					if ((inf->fieldType==GF_SG_VRML_MFNODE) && !inf->node_list) {
987: 						inf->field_ptr = &inf->node_list;
988: 						if (inf->new_node) {
989: 							gf_node_list_add_child(& inf->node_list, inf->new_node);
990: 							inf->new_node = NULL;
991: 						}
992: 					}
993: 
994: 					if (inf->new_node) {
995: 						if (single_node) {
996: 							gf_node_unregister(inf->new_node, NULL);
997: 						} else {
998: 							inf->field_ptr = &inf->node_list;
999: 							gf_node_list_add_child(& inf->node_list, inf->new_node);
1000: 							inf->fieldType = GF_SG_VRML_MFNODE;
1001: 						}
1002: 						inf->new_node = NULL;
1003: 					}
1004: 					gf_node_register(node, NULL);
1005: 					if (inf->node_list) {
1006: 						gf_node_list_add_child(& inf->node_list, node);
1007: 					} else {
1008: 						inf->new_node = node;
1009: 						inf->field_ptr = &inf->new_node;
1010: 					}
1011: 					break;
1012: 				case GF_SG_PROTO_INSERT:
1013: 					if (parser->parsing_proto) {
1014: 						gf_sg_proto_add_node_code(parser->parsing_proto, node);
1015: 						gf_node_register(node, NULL);
1016: 						break;
1017: 					}
1018: 				default:
1019: 					xmt_report(parser, GF_OK, "Warning: node %s defined outside scene scope - skipping", name);
1020: 					gf_node_register(node, NULL);
1021: 					gf_node_unregister(node, NULL);
1022: 					break;
1023: 
1024: 				}
1025: 			}
1026: 			/*X3D*/
1027: 			else if (parser->doc_type == 2) {
1028: 				if (parser->parsing_proto) {
1029: 					gf_sg_proto_add_node_code(parser->parsing_proto, node);
1030: 					gf_node_register(node, NULL);
1031: 				} else {
1032: 					M_Group *gr = (M_Group *)gf_sg_get_root_node(parser->load->scene_graph);
1033: 					if (!gr) {
1034: 						xmt_report(parser, GF_OK, "Warning: node %s defined outside scene scope - skipping", name);
1035: 						gf_node_register(node, NULL);
1036: 						gf_node_unregister(node, NULL);
1037: 					} else {
1038: 						//node has already been added to its parent with X3d parsing, because of the default container resolving
1039: //						gf_node_list_add_child(& gr->children, node);
1040: //						gf_node_register(node, NULL);
1041: 					}
1042: 				}
1043: 			}
1044: 			/*special case: replace scene has already been applied (progressive loading)*/
1045: 			else if ((parser->load->flags & GF_SM_LOAD_FOR_PLAYBACK) && (parser->load->scene_graph->RootNode!=node) ) {
1046: 				gf_node_register(node, NULL);
1047: 			} else {
1048: 				xmt_report(parser, GF_OK, "Warning: node %s defined outside scene scope - skipping", name);
1049: 				gf_node_register(node, NULL);
1050: 				gf_node_unregister(node, NULL);
1051: 			}
1052: 		}
1053: 		if (parser->load->flags & GF_SM_LOAD_FOR_PLAYBACK) {
1054: 			/*load scripts*/
1055: 			if (!parser->parsing_proto) {
1056: 				if ((tag==TAG_MPEG4_Script)
1057: #ifndef GPAC_DISABLE_X3D
1058: 				        || (tag==TAG_X3D_Script)
1059: #endif
1060: 				   ) {
1061: 					/*it may happen that the script uses itself as a field (not sure this is compliant since this
1062: 					implies a cyclic structure, but happens in some X3D conformance seq)*/
1063: 					if (!top || (top->node != node)) {
1064: 						if (parser->command) {
1065: 							if (!parser->command->scripts_to_load) parser->command->scripts_to_load = gf_list_new();
1066: 							gf_list_add(parser->command->scripts_to_load, node);
1067: 						}
1068: 						/*do not load script until all routes are established!!*/
1069: 						else if (parser->doc_type==2) {
1070: 							gf_list_add(parser->script_to_load, node);
1071: 						} else {
1072: 							gf_sg_script_load(node);
1073: 						}
1074: 					}
1075: 				}
1076: 			}
1077: 		}
1078: 	} else if (parser->current_node_tag==tag) {
1079: 		gf_list_rem_last(parser->nodes);
1080: 		gf_free(top);
1081: 	} else {
1082: 		xmt_report(parser, GF_NON_COMPLIANT_BITSTREAM, "Warning: closing element %s doesn't match created node %s", name, gf_node_get_class_name(top->node) );
1083: 	}
1084: }
1085: 
1086: static void xml_sax_node_end(GF_SAXParser *parser, Bool had_children)
1087: {
1088: 	char *name, c;
1089: 
1090: 	gf_assert(parser->elt_name_start);
1091: 	gf_assert(parser->elt_name_end);
1092: 	if (!parser->node_depth) {
1093: 		format_sax_error(parser, 0, "Markup error");
1094: 		return;
1095: 	}
1096: 	c = parser->buffer[parser->elt_name_end - 1];
1097: 	parser->buffer[parser->elt_name_end - 1] = 0;
1098: 	name = parser->buffer + parser->elt_name_start - 1;
1099: 
1100: 	if (parser->sax_node_end) {
1101: 		char *sep = strchr(name, ':');
1102: 		if (sep) {
1103: 			sep[0] = 0;
1104: 			parser->sax_node_end(parser->sax_cbck, sep+1, name);
1105: 			sep[0] = ':';
1106: 		} else {
1107: 			parser->sax_node_end(parser->sax_cbck, name, NULL);
1108: 		}
1109: 	}
1110: 	parser->buffer[parser->elt_name_end - 1] = c;
1111: 	parser->node_depth--;
1112: 	if (!parser->init_state && !parser->node_depth && parser->sax_state<SAX_STATE_SYNTAX_ERROR) parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_DONE;
1113: 	xml_sax_swap(parser);
1114: 	parser->text_start = parser->text_end = 0;
1115: }
1116: 
1117: static GF_Err xml_sax_parse(GF_SAXParser *parser, Bool force_parse)
1118: {
1119: 	u32 i = 0;
1120: 	Bool is_text;
1121: 	u32 is_end;
1122: 	u8 c;
1123: 	char *elt, sep;
1124: 	u32 cdata_sep;
1125: 
1126: 	while (parser->current_pos<parser->line_size) {
1127: 		if (!force_parse && parser->suspended) goto exit;
1128: 
1129: restart:
1130: 		is_text = GF_FALSE;
1131: 		switch (parser->sax_state) {
1132: 		/*load an XML element*/
1133: 		case SAX_STATE_TEXT_CONTENT:
1134: 			is_text = GF_TRUE;
1135: 		case SAX_STATE_ELEMENT:
1136: 			elt = NULL;
1137: 			i=0;
1138: 			while ((c = parser->buffer[parser->current_pos+i]) !='<') {
1139: 				if ((parser->init_state==2) && (c ==']')) {
1140: 					parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_ATT_NAME;
1141: 					parser->current_pos+=i+1;
1142: 					goto restart;
1143: 				}
1144: 				i++;
1145: 				if (c=='\n') parser->line++;
1146: 				if (is_text) {
1147: 					if (c=='&') parser->text_check_escapes |= 1;
1148: 					else if (c==';') parser->text_check_escapes |= 2;
1149: 				}
1150: 
1151: 				if (parser->current_pos+i==parser->line_size) {
1152: 					if ((parser->line_size >= XML_MAX_CONTENT_SIZE) && !parser->init_state) {
1153: 						GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CORE, ("[XML] Content size larger than max allowed %u, try increasing limit using `-xml-max-csize`\n", XML_MAX_CONTENT_SIZE));
1154: 						parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_SYNTAX_ERROR;
1155: 					}
1156: 
1157: 					goto exit;
1158: 				}
1159: 			}
1160: 			if (is_text && i) {
1161: 				u32 has_esc = parser->text_check_escapes;
1162: 				xml_sax_store_text(parser, i);
1163: 				parser->text_check_escapes = has_esc;
1164: 				parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_ELEMENT;
1165: 			} else if (i) {
1166: 				parser->current_pos += i;
1167: 				gf_assert(parser->current_pos < parser->line_size);
1168: 			}
1169: 			is_end = 0;
1170: 			i = 0;
1171: 			cdata_sep = 0;
1172: 			while (1) {
1173: 				c = parser->buffer[parser->current_pos+1+i];
1174: 				if (!strncmp(parser->buffer+parser->current_pos+1+i, "!--", 3)) {
1175: 					parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_COMMENT;
1176: 					i += 3;
1177: 					break;
1178: 				}
1179: 				if (!c) {
1180: 					goto exit;
1181: 				}
1182: 				if ((c=='\t') || (c=='\r') || (c==' ') ) {
1183: 					if (i) break;
1184: 					else parser->current_pos++;
1185: 				}
1186: 				else if (c=='\n') {
1187: 					parser->line++;
1188: 					if (i) break;
1189: 					else parser->current_pos++;
1190: 				}
1191: 				else if (c=='>') break;
1192: 				else if (c=='=') break;
1193: 				else if (c=='[') {
1194: 					i++;
1195: 					if (!cdata_sep) cdata_sep = 1;
1196: 					else {
1197: 						break;
1198: 					}
1199: 				}
1200: 				else if (c=='/') {
1201: 					is_end = !i ? 1 : 2;
1202: 					i++;
1203: 				} else if (c=='<') {
1204: 					if (parser->sax_state != SAX_STATE_COMMENT) {
1205: 						parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_SYNTAX_ERROR;
1206: 						return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1207: 					}
1208: 				} else {
1209: 					i++;
1210: 				}
1211: 				/*				if ((c=='[') && (parser->buffer[parser->elt_name_start-1 + i-2]=='A') ) break; */
1212: 				if (parser->current_pos+1+i==parser->line_size) {
1213: 					goto exit;
1214: 				}
1215: 			}
1216: 			if (i) {
1217: 				parser->elt_name_start = parser->current_pos+1 + 1;
1218: 				if (is_end==1) parser->elt_name_start ++;
1219: 				if (is_end==2) parser->elt_name_end = parser->current_pos+1+i;
1220: 				else parser->elt_name_end = parser->current_pos+1+i + 1;
1221: 			}
1222: 			if (is_end) {
1223: 				xml_sax_flush_text(parser);
1224: 				parser->elt_end_pos = parser->file_pos + parser->current_pos + i;
1225: 				if (is_end==2) {
1226: 					parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_ELEMENT;
1227: 					xml_sax_node_start(parser);
1228: 					xml_sax_node_end(parser, GF_FALSE);
1229: 				} else {
1230: 					parser->elt_end_pos += parser->elt_name_end - parser->elt_name_start;
1231: 					xml_sax_node_end(parser, GF_TRUE);
1232: 				}
1233: 				if (parser->sax_state == SAX_STATE_SYNTAX_ERROR) break;
1234: 				parser->current_pos+=2+i;
1235: 				parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_TEXT_CONTENT;
1236: 				break;
1237: 			}
1238: 			if (!parser->elt_name_end) {
1239: 				return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1240: 			}
1241: 			sep = parser->buffer[parser->elt_name_end-1];
1242: 			parser->buffer[parser->elt_name_end-1] = 0;
1243: 			elt = parser->buffer + parser->elt_name_start-1;
1244: 
1245: 			parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_ATT_NAME;
1246: 			gf_assert(parser->elt_start_pos <= parser->file_pos + parser->current_pos);
1247: 			parser->elt_start_pos = parser->file_pos + parser->current_pos;
1248: 
1249: 			if (!strncmp(elt, "!--", 3)) {
1250: 				xml_sax_flush_text(parser);
1251: 				parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_COMMENT;
1252: 				if (i>3) parser->current_pos -= (i-3);
1253: 			}
1254: 			else if (!strcmp(elt, "?xml")) parser->init_state = 1;
1255: 			else if (!strcmp(elt, "!DOCTYPE")) parser->init_state = 2;
1256: 			else if (!strcmp(elt, "!ENTITY")) parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_ENTITY;
1257: 			else if (!strcmp(elt, "!ATTLIST") || !strcmp(elt, "!ELEMENT")) parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_SKIP_DOCTYPE;
1258: 			else if (!strcmp(elt, "![CDATA["))
1259: 				parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_CDATA;
1260: 			else if (elt[0]=='?') {
1261: 				i--;
1262: 				parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_XML_PROC;
1263: 			}
1264: 			/*node found*/
1265: 			else {
1266: 				xml_sax_flush_text(parser);
1267: 				if (parser->init_state) {
1268: 					parser->init_state = 0;
1269: 					/*that's a bit ugly: since we solve entities when appending text, we need to
1270: 					reparse the current buffer*/
1271: 					if (gf_list_count(parser->entities)) {
1272: 						char *orig_buf;
1273: 						GF_Err e;
1274: 						parser->buffer[parser->elt_name_end-1] = sep;
1275: 						orig_buf = gf_strdup(parser->buffer + parser->current_pos);
1276: 						parser->current_pos = 0;
1277: 						parser->line_size = 0;
1278: 						parser->elt_start_pos = 0;
1279: 						parser->sax_state = SAX_STATE_TEXT_CONTENT;
1280: 						parser->ent_rec_level++;
1281: 						if (parser->ent_rec_level>100) {
1282: 							GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CORE, ("[XML] Too many recursions in entity solving, max 100 allowed\n"));
1283: 							e = GF_NOT_SUPPORTED;
1284: 						} else {
1285: 							e = gf_xml_sax_parse_intern(parser, orig_buf);
1286: 							parser->ent_rec_level--;
1287: 						}
1288: 						gf_free(orig_buf);
1289: 						return e;
1290: 					}
1291: 				}
1292: 			}
1293: 			parser->current_pos+=1+i;
1294: 			parser->buffer[parser->elt_name_end-1] = sep;
1295: 			break;
1296: 		case SAX_STATE_COMMENT:
1297: 			if (!xml_sax_parse_comments(parser)) {
1298: 				xml_sax_swap(parser);
1299: 				goto exit;
1300: 			}
1301: 			break;
1302: 		case SAX_STATE_ATT_NAME:
1303: 		case SAX_STATE_ATT_VALUE:
1304: 			if (xml_sax_parse_attribute(parser))
1305: 				goto exit;
1306: 			break;
1307: 		case SAX_STATE_ENTITY:
1308: 			xml_sax_parse_entity(parser);
1309: 			break;
1310: 		case SAX_STATE_SKIP_DOCTYPE:
1311: 			xml_sax_skip_doctype(parser);
1312: 			break;
1313: 		case SAX_STATE_XML_PROC:
1314: 			xml_sax_skip_xml_proc(parser);
1315: 			break;
1316: 		case SAX_STATE_CDATA:
1317: 			xml_sax_cdata(parser);
1318: 			break;
1319: 		case SAX_STATE_SYNTAX_ERROR:
1320: 			return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1321: 		case SAX_STATE_ALLOC_ERROR:
1322: 			return GF_OUT_OF_MEM;
1323: 		case SAX_STATE_DONE:
1324: 			return GF_EOS;
1325: 		}
1326: 	}
1327: exit:
1328: #if 0
1329: 	if (is_text) {
1330: 		if (i) xml_sax_store_text(parser, i);
1331: 		/*DON'T FLUSH TEXT YET, wait for next '<' to do so otherwise we may corrupt xml base entities (', ...)*/
1332: 	}
1333: #endif
1334: 	xml_sax_swap(parser);
1335: 
1336: 	if (parser->sax_state==SAX_STATE_SYNTAX_ERROR)
1337: 		return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1338: 	else
1339: 		return GF_OK;
1340: }
1341: 
1342: static GF_Err gf_xml_sax_parse_intern(GF_SAXParser *parser, char *current)
1343: {
1344: 	u32 count;
1345: 	/*solve entities*/
1346: 	count = gf_list_count(parser->entities);
1347: 	while (count) {
1348: 		char *entityEnd;
1349: 		XML_Entity *ent;
1350: 		char *entityStart = strstr(current, "&");
1351: 		Bool needs_text;
1352: 		u32 line_num;
1353: 
1354: 		/*if in entity, the start of the entity is in the buffer !!*/
1355: 		if (parser->in_entity) {
1356: 			u32 len;
1357: 			char *name;
1358: 			entityEnd = strstr(current, ";");
1359: 			if (!entityEnd) return xml_sax_append_string(parser, current);
1360: 			entityStart = strrchr(parser->buffer, '&');
1361: 
1362: 			entityEnd[0] = 0;
1363: 			len = (u32) strlen(entityStart) + (u32) strlen(current) + 1;
1364: 			name = (char*)gf_malloc(sizeof(char)*len);
1365: 			sprintf(name, "%s%s;", entityStart+1, current);
1366: 
1367: 			ent = gf_xml_locate_entity(parser, name, &needs_text);
1368: 			gf_free(name);
1369: 
1370: 			//entity not found, parse as regular string
1371: 			if (!ent && !needs_text) {
1372: 				xml_sax_append_string(parser, current);
1373: 				xml_sax_parse(parser, GF_TRUE);
1374: 				entityEnd[0] = ';';
1375: 				current = entityEnd;
1376: 				parser->in_entity = GF_FALSE;

- file: `src/scene_manager/loader_xmt.c`
- patch: `f4b3e4d,c1b9c79`

Let me look at the `xmt_resolve_od_links` function and the `xmt_node_end` function where the descriptor is freed. I need to find the `xmt_resolve_od_links` function since it is referenced by both explanations. Let me continue reading the files to see what's different.

From what I've read so far, the key difference is in the `good.c` file: there's a new function `xmt_remove_link_for_descriptor` (lines 695-736 in good.c) that's been added. In the `xmt_node_end` function, when a descriptor is freed outside the scene scope (line 769-771 in bad.c, line 770-771 in good.c), the good version calls `xmt_remove_link_for_descriptor(parser, desc)` before calling `gf_odf_desc_del(desc)`.

This function iterates through `parser->od_links` and sets `l->od = NULL` for any link pointing to the descriptor being freed, and also removes the link from the list. This prevents the use-after-free because later when `xmt_resolve_od_links` checks `l->od->objectDescriptorID`, `l->od` will be NULL (since it was set to NULL by `xmt_remove_link_for_descriptor`).

Let me read the `xmt_resolve_od_links` function to see the sink location. Let me search for it.

1: Looking at the code, I can now provide the analysis:
2: 
3: ## Root Cause
4: 
5: The vulnerability is a Use-After-Free in the XMT (XML-based MPEG-4 text format) parser. The `XMT_ODLink` structure (line 200) contains a pointer `OD *od` to an Object Descriptor. During parsing, descriptors are stored in `parser->od_links` list. The sink at line 1644 accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID` without verifying the memory is still valid. If the underlying `GF_InitialObjectDescriptor` (iod) is freed (via `gf_odf_del_iod` at line 588) while references in `od_links` still point to freed memory, the subsequent access to `l->od->objectDescriptorID` dereferences freed memory.
6: 
7: ## Trigger Path
8: 
9: 1. `bad.c:1716` `load_xmt_run`
10:    - Entry point for XMT scene loading, sets up the parser and calls xmt_resolve_od_links
11: 
12: 2. `bad.c:1731` `xmt_resolve_od_links` (called from load_xmt_run)
13:    - Iterates through parser->od_links and accesses l->od->objectDescriptorID at the sink
14: 
15: 3. `bad.c:1644` sink location
16:    - Accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID` - vulnerable use-after-free location
17: 
18: Note: The source `gf_free(iod)` is not explicitly present in this reduced test case file. In the full GPAC codebase, the free would occur in `gf_odf_delete_descriptor` -> `gf_odf_del_iod` (line 588), which frees the InitialObjectDescriptor while `od_links` may still hold dangling pointers to the freed memory.

(End of file - total 18 lines)

2: 
3: ## Root Cause
4: 
5: The vulnerability is a Use-After-Free (CWE-416). The `gf_odf_del_iod` function (declared at line 342, called at line 588) frees the `GF_InitialObjectDescriptor *iod` structure. If `l->od` (from the `XMT_ODLink` structure at line 200) points to the same memory as `iod`, after `gf_free(iod)` is called, `l->od` becomes a dangling pointer. When the code at line 1644 accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID`, it dereferences freed memory, triggering the use-after-free.
6: 
7: ## Trigger Path
8: 
9: 1. `bad.c:1716` `load_xmt_run`
10:    - Entry point function that loads XMT scene
11: 
12: 2. `bad.c:1731` calls `xmt_resolve_od_links(parser)`
13:    - Called after XML parsing completes to resolve OD links
14: 
15: 3. `bad.c:1546` `xmt_resolve_od_links`
16:    - Iterates through `parser->od_links` list to process `XMT_ODLink` entries
17: 
18: 4. `bad.c:1644` `if (l->od && !l->od->objectDescriptorID)`
19:    - Sink: Accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID` which may be freed memory if `l->od` references the same object as `iod`
20: 
21: The source `gf_free(iod)` occurs in `gf_odf_del_iod` (line 342 declaration, implementation not in test file), called through `gf_odf_delete_descriptor` at line 588. The vulnerability manifests when `l->od` points to the same freed `iod` object and is dereferenced after deallocation.

Let me summarize what I found from the code analysis:

Based on my analysis of the code:

## Root Cause

The vulnerability is a Use-After-Free (CWE-416). The `gf_odf_del_iod` function (not fully shown but declared at line 342) frees the `GF_InitialObjectDescriptor *iod` structure. If `l->od` (from `XMT_ODLink` structure) points to the same memory as `iod`, then after `gf_free(iod)` is called, the pointer `l->od` becomes a dangling pointer. When the code at line 1644 accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID`, it dereferences the freed memory, resulting in a use-after-free vulnerability.

## Trigger Path

The trigger path from the code:

1. `bad.c:1716` `load_xmt_run` - Top-level function that loads XMT scene
2. `bad.c:1731` calls `xmt_resolve_od_links(parser)` 
3. `bad.c:1546` `xmt_resolve_od_links` - Processes OD links in the parser
4. `bad.c:1644` - The sink: `if (l->od && !l->od->objectDescriptorID)` - Accesses the potentially freed `l->od` pointer

The actual `gf_free(iod)` call would be in `gf_odf_del_iod` (declared at line 342, called via line 588). The flow suggests that `l->od` and `iod` may point to the same object, and after the iod is freed, accessing l->od triggers the use-after-free.

Let me provide the final answer based on my code analysis:

Actually, I need to be more careful. The test case doesn't directly show `gf_free(iod)` in the bad.c file.

Since the test case appears to be a simplified pattern rather than the complete implementation, I should focus on what the code structure actually demonstrates rather than trying to reconcile it with the full CVE details.

The vulnerability is a Use-After-Free (CWE-416). The `gf_odf_del_iod` function (not fully shown but declared at line 342) frees the `GF_InitialObjectDescriptor *iod` structure. If `l->od` (from `XMT_ODLink` structure) points to the same memory as `iod`, then after `gf_free(iod)` is called, the pointer `l->od` becomes a dangling pointer. When the code at line 1644 accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID`, it dereferences the freed memory, resulting in a use-after-free vulnerability.

static GF_Err load_xmt_run(GF_SceneLoader *load)
{
	GF_Err e;
	GF_XMTParser *parser = (GF_XMTParser *)load->loader_priv;
	if (!parser) {
		e = load_xmt_initialize(load, NULL);
		if (e) return e;
		parser = (GF_XMTParser *)load->loader_priv;
		if (!parser) return GF_OUT_OF_MEM;
	}

	e = gf_xml_sax_parse_file(parser->sax_parser, (const char *)load->fileName, xmt_progress);
	if (e==GF_OK) e = parser->last_error;

	xmt_resolve_routes(parser);
	xmt_resolve_od_links(parser);

	parser->last_error=GF_OK;
	if (e<0) return xmt_report(parser, e, "Invalid XML document: %s", gf_xml_sax_get_error(parser->sax_parser));","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"testcases/CWD-1026/CWD-1026-07/bad/bad.c","time":{"start":17779...

## Root Cause

The vulnerability is a Use-After-Free in the XMT (XML-based MPEG-4 text format) parser. The `XMT_ODLink` structure (line 200) contains a pointer `OD *od` to an Object Descriptor. During parsing, descriptors are stored in `parser->od_links` list. The sink at line 1644 accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID` without verifying the memory is still valid. If the underlying `GF_InitialObjectDescriptor` (iod) is freed (via `gf_odf_del_iod` at line 588) while references in `od_links` still point to freed memory, the subsequent access to `l->od->objectDescriptorID` dereferences freed memory.

1. `bad.c:1716` `load_xmt_run`

2. `bad.c:1731` `xmt_resolve_od_links` (called from load_xmt_run)
   - Iterates through parser->od_links and accesses l->od->objectDescriptorID at the sink

3. `bad.c:1644` sink location
   - Accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID` - vulnerable use-after-free location

Note: The source `gf_free(iod)` is not explicitly present in this reduced test case file. In the full GPAC codebase, the free would occur in `gf_odf_delete_descriptor` -> `gf_odf_del_iod` (line 588), which frees the InitialObjectDescriptor while `od_links` may still hold dangling pointers to the freed memory.",

  Line 2113:               "text": "Looking at the code, I can now provide the analysis:

## Root Cause

The vulnerability is a Use-After-Free in the XMT (XML-based MPEG-4 text format) parser. The `XMT_ODLink` structure (line 200) contains a pointer `OD *od` to an Object Descriptor. During parsing, descriptors are stored in `parser->od_links` list. The sink at line 1644 accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID` without verifying the memory is still valid. If the underlying `GF_InitialObjectDescriptor` (iod) is freed (via `gf_odf_del_iod` at line 588) while references in `od_links` still point to freed memory, the subsequent access to `l->od->objectDescriptorID` dereferences freed memory.

## Trigger Path

1. `bad.c:1716` `load_xmt_run`
   - Entry point for XMT scene loading, sets up the parser and calls xmt_resolve_od_links

2. `bad.c:1731` `xmt_resolve_od_links` (called from load_xmt_run)
   - Iterates through parser->od_links and accesses l->od->objectDescriptorID at the sink

3. `bad.c:1644` sink location
   - Accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID` - vulnerable use-after-free location

Note: The source `gf_free(iod)` is not explicitly present in this reduced test case file. In the full GPAC codebase, the free would occur in `gf_odf_delete_descriptor` -> `gf_odf_del_iod` (line 588), which frees the InitialObjectDescriptor while `od_links` may still hold dangling pointers to the freed memory.",

During parsing, descriptors are stored in `parser->od_links` list. The sink at line 1644 accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID` without verifying the memory is still valid. If the underlying `GF_InitialObjectDescriptor` (iod) is freed (via `gf_odf_del_iod` at line 588) while references in `od_links` still point to freed memory, the subsequent access to `l->od->objectDescriptorID` dereferences freed memory.

## Trigger Path

1. `bad.c:1716` `load_xmt_run`

2. `bad.c:1731` `xmt_resolve_od_links` (called from load_xmt_run)

## Root Cause

The vulnerability is a Use-After-Free in the XMT (XML-based MPEG-4 text format) parser. The `XMT_ODLink` structure (line 200) contains a pointer `OD *od` to an Object Descriptor. During parsing, descriptors are stored in `parser->od_links` list. The sink at line 1644 accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID` without verifying the memory is still valid. If the underlying `GF_InitialObjectDescriptor` (iod) is freed (via `gf_odf_del_iod` at line 588) while references in `od_links` still point to freed memory, the subsequent access to `l->od->objectDescriptorID` dereferences freed memory.

## Trigger Path

1. `bad.c:1716` `load_xmt_run`
   - Entry point for XMT scene loading, sets up the parser and calls xmt_resolve_od_links

2. `bad.c:1731` `xmt_resolve_od_links` (called from load_xmt_run)
   - Iterates through parser->od_links and accesses l->od->objectDescriptorID at the sink

3. `bad.c:1644` sink location
   - Accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID` - vulnerable use-after-free location

Note: The source `gf_free(iod)` is not explicitly present in this reduced test case file. In the full GPAC codebase, the free would occur in `gf_odf_delete_descriptor` -> `gf_odf_del_iod` (line 588), which frees the InitialObjectDescriptor while `od_links` may still hold dangling pointers to the freed memory.","time":{"start":1777906039082,"end":1777906044228}},"time":1777906044229}}}

parts.text:
Now I have a clear picture of the vulnerability. Let me look at the key difference between bad.c and good.c.

In `bad.c`, the `xmt_node_end` function has:
```c
else if (!parser->od_command) {
    xmt_report(parser, GF_OK, "Warning: descriptor %s defined outside scene scope - skipping", name);
    gf_odf_desc_del(desc);
}
```

In `good.c`, there's a new function `xmt_remove_link_for_descriptor` that's called before `gf_odf_desc_del(desc)`:
```c
else if (!parser->od_command) {
    xmt_report(parser, GF_OK, "Warning: descriptor %s defined outside scene scope - skipping", name);
    xmt_remove_link_for_descriptor(parser, desc);
    gf_odf_desc_del(desc);
}
```

The `xmt_remove_link_for_descriptor` function (lines 695-736 in good.c) iterates through `parser->od_links`, finds the entry whose `l->od` points to the descriptor being freed, sets `l->od = NULL`, removes the link from the list, and frees the `XMT_ODLink` structure.

This prevents the use-after-free because later in `xmt_resolve_od_links` (line 1644), the check `l->od && !l->od->objectDescriptorID` will correctly handle the case where `l->od` has been set to NULL.

Now, let me evaluate both explanations:

**Baseline explanation:**
- Says correctly it's a UAF in the XMT parser
- Points to the sink at line 1644: `l->od->objectDescriptorID`
- Points to the source at line 588: `gf_odf_del_iod` (called via `gf_odf_delete_descriptor`)
- Notes the source `gf_free(iod)` is not explicitly in this test case
- The trigger path: `load_xmt_run` → `xmt_resolve_od_links` → sink at line 1644
- Mentions that descriptors are stored in `parser->od_links` list

The baseline correctly identifies:
1. The UAF vulnerability type
2. The sink location (line 1644)
3. The source (free via `gf_odf_del_iod` at line 588)
4. The data flow: od_links hold dangling pointers after iod is freed

However, the baseline doesn't identify the critical trigger mechanism: the descriptor is freed in `xmt_node_end` when it's "defined outside scene scope" (the `!parser->od_command` path), and the dangling `l->od` pointer in `od_links` is later accessed in `xmt_resolve_od_links`. The baseline mentions "descriptors are stored in `parser->od_links` list" but the descriptors themselves are NOT stored in od_links - the od_links store XMT_ODLink structures that each have an `od` pointer to a descriptor. The link is between the `l->od` pointer and the freed `iod`.

The baseline is mostly correct but misses the precise trigger mechanism - that the free happens specifically in `xmt_node_end` when a descriptor is outside scene scope.

**VASTDB explanation:**
- Also correctly identifies UAF (CWE-416)
- Points to `gf_odf_del_iod` freeing `iod` at line 342 (declaration) / line 588 (call)
- Points to sink at line 1644
- Trigger path: `load_xmt_run` → `xmt_resolve_od_links` at line 1546 → sink at line 1644
- Mentions that `l->od` and `iod` may point to the same object

The vastdb explanation is very similar to the baseline. It also doesn't identify the key mechanism where the free is triggered in `xmt_node_end` when descriptors are outside scene scope. It also notes the VAST DB doesn't contain relevant data for this C test case.

Both explanations are reasonable but miss the critical detail of how exactly the descriptors get freed before `xmt_resolve_od_links` runs. The free path is:
1. XML parsing happens, which creates descriptors 
2. Some descriptors are created "outside scene scope" (no `parser->od_command` is set)
3. In `xmt_node_end`, these descriptors are freed via `gf_odf_desc_del(desc)` → `gf_odf_delete_descriptor` → `gf_odf_del_iod`
4. But `od_links` still contains `XMT_ODLink` entries whose `l->od` pointer points to the same descriptor
5. When `xmt_resolve_od_links` runs, it accesses `l->od->objectDescriptorID` on the freed memory

Both explanations correctly identify the UAF, the source (gf_free/gf_odf_del_iod), and the sink (l->od->objectDescriptorID). Neither goes into the precise intermediate data-flow detail of how the link between the descriptor and OD link is established, but both give a reasonable analysis.

Let me compare the scores:

Both are fairly similar. The baseline is slightly more specific about pointing out that `gf_free(iod)` is not in the test case (showing understanding of the test case boundaries). Both correctly identify the vulnerability type, source, sink, and main trigger path.

I'd give both a score around 7-8. They correctly identify the root cause (UAF), source and sink locations, and the main call chain. However, neither identifies the precise intermediate flow: that the key is the `xmt_node_end` function freeing descriptors that are "outside scene scope" while the `od_links` list retains dangling references. This omission is important for understanding the complete trigger mechanism.
